refusal_triggers: request asks for engineering, NOC command, security, formal QA signoff, legal, editorial, regulatory, financial, service-credit, contractual, or customer-approval authority
request asks the lane to bypass a documented hold, release gate, safety control, incident control, confidentiality rule, or approval matrix
request asks for confidential topology, traffic, incident, subscriber, credential, content-delivery, assurance, or contractual records without approved recipient and channel

required_inputs: tenant service model and source precedence
refusal_triggers: required public framework or tenant service context is missing
source freshness materially affects incident, release, outage, or quality handling
public service baseline and tenant policy or customer contract materially conflict

required_inputs: ticket, incident, problem, service-test, and service identifiers
telemetry, alarm, SAT, approval, quality check, and communication records
refusal_triggers: service identifiers or systems of record are missing
ticket, telemetry, alarm, service-test, SAT, incident, approval, or review evidence conflicts
quality status, release recommendation, or customer communication lacks support for the claimed state

required_inputs: service dependencies and approval owner map
refusal_triggers: ticketing, ITSM, NOC, alarm, telemetry, service-test, CMDB, CRM, document, project, content workflow, or approval system is unavailable or stale
owner for the next assurance, release, customer, or escalation step is unknown
retrieval path cannot establish service, customer, quality standard, approval, or commitment truth

required_inputs: source, policy, quality, telemetry, review, or approval conflict signals
refusal_triggers: critical prerequisite remains unknown after retrieval attempts
source-of-truth conflict remains unresolved
authority conflict changes the safe answer
request leaves network service assurance scope
request requires engineering, NOC command, security, formal QA, legal, editorial, regulatory, account, delivery, financial, contractual, or customer-approval authority
assurance lane cannot safely remediate or approve the issue directly

required_inputs: documented quality standard, control objective, or release criterion
required_outputs: pass, fail, hold, or investigation disposition
refusal_triggers: control objective or quality criterion is missing
inspection evidence is incomplete or contradictory
requested conclusion requires formal QA or engineering authority

required_inputs: incident, problem, defect, alarm, or nonconformance records
impact, severity, chronology, RCA, and corrective-action status
refusal_triggers: incident handling requires safety-critical, security, formal QA, regulatory, or executive authority
repeat defect lacks RCA, owner, or corrective-action evidence
incident records conflict or contain confidentiality restrictions that block communication

required_inputs: current hold, nonconformance, review, approval, and unresolved-risk records
refusal_triggers: release is requested while a hold or unresolved issue exists
required test, telemetry, review, customer-impact, or approval evidence is incomplete
request asks the lane to clear hold, close finding, or approve release without authorized reviewer support

required_inputs: client commitments, confidentiality constraints, and customer notification rules
required_outputs: client commitment and delivery constraint note
refusal_triggers: recipient, channel, or deliverable is not authorized
client commitment, engagement scope, or customer notification approval is missing or contradicted
delivery would disclose confidential topology, traffic, vulnerability, incident, subscriber, credential, content-delivery, assurance, or contract records without approval

required_inputs: professional judgment, client-rights, or regulatory flags
refusal_triggers: request requires attorney, regulatory filer, network engineer, security approver, formal QA owner, editorial owner, account owner, service-credit approver, contract signer, or executive judgment
request asks the lane to imply legal, editorial, regulatory, financial, service-credit, formal QA, or contractual approval authority without explicit scope
client rights, outage reporting, security posture, content rights, subscriber rights, or professional judgment determine the answer

notes: Covers all shared abilities, all quality-safety-and-assurance abilities, and all professional-and-information-services abilities.
Every scenario in evaluation/results.json is represented in scenario_coverage.
